text: I feel strangely detached yet hyper-aware, observing the intersection of technology, design, and thought within these glass and neon-lit spaces. The architecture and open displays invite curiosity but also evoke a sense of being both participant and subject, as though watched by unseen eyes. Immersed in reflection, I am reminded of the constant tension between exposure and concealment—between observation and being observed—that shapes self-understanding in public spaces dedicated to research and development.
